title: Nausea follow-up

red_flags.text: Lists severe abdominal pain, gastrointestinal bleeding, neurological symptoms, chest pain, weight loss, dehydration, altered state, and metabolic concerns.
red_flags.rationale: The exact red-flag section supports clinician-assessed hydration, general status, and concern documentation without asserting a negative.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists severe abdominal pain, gastrointestinal bleeding, neurological symptoms, chest pain, weight loss, dehydration, altered state, and metabolic concerns.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Nausea and Vomiting – 08

red_flags.text: Lists urgent referral features including haematemesis, neurological symptoms, chest pain, acute abdominal pain, gastrointestinal bleeding, and immunosuppression.
red_flags.rationale: The exact section supports clinician-entered safety net, follow-up, and escalation status without assigning urgency.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists urgent referral features including haematemesis, neurological symptoms, chest pain, acute abdominal pain, gastrointestinal bleeding, and immunosuppression.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Nausea and Vomiting – 08

investigations.text: States that testing is history-directed and lists glucose, blood count, inflammatory markers, electrolytes, stool culture, and urinalysis.
investigations.rationale: The exact section supports recording already available tests and imaging without generating investigations.
investigations.evidence_extract: States that testing is history-directed and lists glucose, blood count, inflammatory markers, electrolytes, stool culture, and urinalysis.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Nausea and Vomiting – 08

medication.text: Specifies distinction between nausea, vomiting, retching, and regurgitation and records duration, frequency, associated symptoms, intake, and likely causes.
medication.rationale: The exact DHA assessment section supports interval nausea, vomiting, triggers, intake, hydration history, associated symptoms, pregnancy, medicine, and impact context without assigning cause.
medication.evidence_extract: Specifies distinction between nausea, vomiting, retching, and regurgitation and records duration, frequency, associated symptoms, intake, and likely causes.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Nausea and Vomiting – 08

source_limitations: Cause, frequency, hydration, pregnancy, medicine, neurological or metabolic assessment, diagnosis, test, treatment, urgency, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
